title: Determinants of variability in motor performance in middle childhood: a cross-sectional study of balance and motor co-ordination skills
abstract: The current study provides preliminary evidence of motor performance from a typically developing rural population within an age range that has not been previously studied. As well as being culturally appropriate, the developed tests were reliable, valid and sensitive to biological and environmental correlates. Further, the use of composite scores seems to strengthen the magnitude of differences seen among groups.
